criteria.label: Define loss surface / loss landscape and identify its axes in words.
criteria.example: State: horizontal axes are parameters θ, vertical axis is L(θ), and one point is one full model configuration with its training loss.

criteria.label: Sketch or interpret a 2D contour plot of a convex bowl.
criteria.example: For L = w₁² + 4w₂², draw nested elliptical contours centered at (0,0) and note steeper ascent along w₂ than w₁.

criteria.label: Distinguish local minimum, global minimum, and saddle on a loss surface.
criteria.example: Give a 1D example: L=x⁴−x² has saddles at x=0 (not a min) and local minima at x=±1/√2.

criteria.label: Explain why a training loss curve alone is not the full loss surface.
criteria.example: Say: the curve is loss only along the optimizer's time path θₜ; it omits height at unvisited parameter points in other directions.

criteria.label: Relate loss surface geometry to gradient descent behavior (plateau, overshoot).
criteria.example: Describe: on a plateau ||∇L|| is tiny so steps barely reduce loss; with LR too large in a narrow valley, updates oscillate across the bowl.
